title: DoorDash shares pop 13% on second-quarter revenue beat
text: DoorDash's shares popped 13% in extended trading after reporting second-quarter results that beat analysts' revenue expectations. The company reported revenue of $2.63 billion versus the expected $2.54 billion, with revenue increasing 23% year-over-year. DoorDash received 635 million total orders during Q2 2024, up 19% year-over-year. However, the company reported a GAAP net loss of $158 million, slightly better than the $172 million loss in Q2 2023 but missing the estimated EPS. Net Revenue Margin increased to 13.3% from 13.0% in Q2 2023. Adjusted EBITDA increased 54% year-over-year to $430 million. These results demonstrated DoorDash's strong operational execution with significant year-over-year growth in both orders and revenue, supporting the company's path toward sustained profitability despite near-term EPS misses.
